text.title: GDPR DSAR Runbook — Product Dev Team Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of gdpr-dsar-runbook-product-dev-team enforces. Each rule has a statement, a rationale anchored to industry practice, and a source citation.

rule.statement: DSAR is delivered within 30 days of intake unless complexity triggers a lawful extension (max 2 additional months, notified to subject in writing).
rule.rationale: Article 12(3) sets the timeline; missing it is a notifiable breach risk.

rule.statement: Identity is verified before any personal data ships — using independent evidence not available to attackers via the public account.
rule.rationale: Failure here is a data breach via DSAR (regulators have fined for this).

rule.statement: Every system listed in the data map is queried; absence MUST be justified in writing as 'no personal data of this subject'.

rationale.source: EDPB guidelines on right of access 2022/01

rule.rationale: Missing-system DSARs are the dominant complaint to supervisory authorities.

rule.statement: Redaction removes third-party personal data, internal trade secrets, and source-system credentials BEFORE delivery.
rule.rationale: Unredacted bulk dumps create new violations (third-party PII disclosure).

rule.statement: Every DSAR is logged with audit trail: timeline, systems queried, redactions applied, deliverer identity.
rule.rationale: The audit trail is the regulator-facing evidence; absent it, the controller is presumed non-compliant.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `Applies If` are not all true, skip this methodology and route to the adjacent one referenced in Related.
rule.rationale: A skip rule keeps the decision tree closed: every branch ends at a real rule id.
